global_rules: This tranche modifies the current deterministic cognition architecture and does not authorise a rewrite.
One working branch per gate; no downstream gate begins until the current gate is complete, green, and merged.
DMP v1 remains the live binding default unless a dedicated gate explicitly proves and promotes a DMP v2 migration.
Modules remain interchangeable only within their grammar slot.
Shared primitives are allowed; downstream verdict loops are forbidden.
Weekend / overnight / event carry remains a distinct horizon branch.
Raw and derived surfaces remain separated.

leaves.title: Pin stage ownership for the cognitive workflow modification
leaves.scope: Map each workflow branch from calendar/horizon through review/explanation to its owning stage, including explicit boundaries and non-goals.
implementation_tasks: Write a stage-ownership map for calendar/horizon, temporal phase, regime, options/flow, posture/risk, candidate families, setup variants, execution expression, carry branch, and review.
State explicitly which stage owns candidate family generation.
State explicitly what may and may not flow into the carry branch.
Record non-goals so the tranche cannot expand into a rewrite.
validation_tasks: Check the implementation map against docs/01_NORMATIVE.md and docs/02_OPERATING_MODEL.md.
Ensure no stage ownership contradicts the audit findings.
leaves.pass_gate: The workflow implementation map is explicit, bounded, and normatively aligned.
leaves.merge_gate: The map does not contradict the current seven-stage runtime order unless such a change is explicitly justified and proven.

leaves.title: Freeze candidate-generation and carry-branch boundaries
leaves.scope: Prevent drift by pinning where candidate family generation ends and where carry-horizon decisioning begins.
implementation_tasks: Define the boundary between posture/risk permission and candidate family generation.
Define the boundary between execution expression and carry-horizon handoff.
State explicit examples of invalid cross-stage leakage.
validation_tasks: Ensure the boundary rules do not reintroduce downstream-verdict loops into Step 1 or carry routing.
leaves.pass_gate: Boundaries are explicit enough that later runtime implementation cannot reasonably claim ambiguity.
leaves.merge_gate: Boundary rules are consistent with audit findings and normative docs.

leaves.title: Pin calendar/horizon ownership and boundary semantics
leaves.scope: Make Step 0 calendar/horizon ownership explicit so intraday evaluation, carry evaluation, and out-of-hours classification cannot drift between temporal and carry code paths.
implementation_tasks: State which existing runtime stage owns Step 0 calendar/horizon classification.
Define how intraday vs overnight/weekend/event-carry evaluation is selected before normal stage traversal.
State explicitly that Step 0 does not create a hidden eighth runtime stage unless separately proposed and proven.
validation_tasks: Ensure calendar/horizon ownership is explicit and does not conflict with the seven-stage runtime order.
Ensure carry entry conditions and out-of-hours evaluation rules are no longer implicit.
leaves.pass_gate: Calendar/horizon ownership is explicit and cannot be confused with temporal-state or carry logic.
leaves.merge_gate: Step 0 is pinned without silently creating a new runtime stage or hidden routing path.

leaves.title: Refactor registry contracts to native family/variant/expression hierarchy
leaves.scope: Upgrade the registry contracts so the runtime speaks native family, setup variant, execution expression, and horizon rather than treating hierarchy as a cosmetic wrapper.
implementation_tasks: Promote family, setup_variant, execution_expression, and horizon to native first-class contract fields.
Document compatibility behaviour for existing live rules.
Update the registry spec so it no longer reads as a bridge-only surface.
validation_tasks: Prove the registry schema serialises and validates cleanly.
Prove the checked-in registry file matches the documented schema expectations.
leaves.pass_gate: The registry contracts are natively hierarchical and explicitly versioned.
leaves.merge_gate: Legacy compatibility remains explicit and tested rather than accidental.

leaves.title: Move runtime eligibility from flat rule map toward hierarchical evaluation
leaves.scope: Refactor runtime selection so the eligibility layer evaluates family/variant/expression natively instead of living mostly inside a hard-coded rule-id map.
implementation_tasks: Introduce a family-first selection path in runtime logic.
Ensure review/explanation can reconstruct chosen family, setup variant, and execution expression.
Preserve deterministic ordering and inspectability.
validation_tasks: Prove current live rules still select correctly under the compatibility bridge.
Prove explanation lineage includes the hierarchical path.
leaves.pass_gate: Runtime evaluation is natively hierarchical without losing deterministic selection.
leaves.merge_gate: Current live playbooks remain explainable and compatibility-tested.

leaves.title: Expand candidate-family scaffolding without uncontrolled taxonomy bloat
leaves.scope: Add bounded scaffolding for the expanded family library discussed in the workflow update without pretending all future trader setups are implemented immediately.
implementation_tasks: Record the 12-family scaffold and mark which families are implemented, partially scaffolded, or planned only.
Prevent the registry from implying unsupported runtime depth.
Document which overlays remain sub-variants rather than top-level families.
validation_tasks: Ensure no family is marked live without a runtime-supported path.
leaves.pass_gate: The family library is richer on paper without overstating what runtime supports today.
leaves.merge_gate: Registry and docs do not claim live support for unimplemented families.

leaves.title: Define the typed close-state to carry-state handoff contract
leaves.scope: Pin the exact typed packet that passes close-state cognition outputs into overnight/weekend/event carry decisioning.
implementation_tasks: Define the handoff schema from intraday close-state into carry evaluation.
Specify required and optional fields explicitly.
Define the held-position / inventory fields required for carry decisions; do not assume carry can be evaluated from phase alone.
Document prohibited implicit carry assumptions.
validation_tasks: Prove handoff serialisation and validation.
Prove weekend/event branches read the contract consistently.
leaves.pass_gate: Carry handoff is typed, explicit, and documented.
leaves.merge_gate: The handoff contract is sufficient for weekend and event-carry routing without borrowing hidden intraday state.

leaves.title: Freeze weekend, overnight, and event-carry taxonomy
leaves.scope: Define the horizon taxonomy so Friday/weekend/event logic cannot drift back into generic late-session semantics.
implementation_tasks: Define ordinary overnight, weekend carry, and event carry categories.
State exactly when weekend_window applies.
State what is and is not a valid event-carry state.
validation_tasks: Prove taxonomy labels are used consistently across carry code and docs.
leaves.pass_gate: Weekend and event carry are no longer semantically ambiguous.
leaves.merge_gate: Carry taxonomy is explicit enough to support explanation and replay.

leaves.title: Pin allowed carry actions and downgrade/override rules
leaves.scope: Define allowed carry actions and the downgrade rules that convert posture and carry context into final allowed carry behaviour.
implementation_tasks: Define allowed actions such as flatten, hold baseline, reduce, add, and block carry.
Document downgrade/override rules for weekend and event cases.
Ensure explanation outputs reflect the final action actually permitted.
validation_tasks: Prove allowed-action lists are deduplicated and coherent.
Prove recommendation/action mismatches cannot silently persist.
leaves.pass_gate: Carry actions and downgrade rules are deterministic and explanation-safe.
leaves.merge_gate: Weekend/event downgrade paths are explicitly tested and green.

leaves.title: Inventory all live DMP binding surfaces
leaves.scope: Create a precise inventory of live DMP v1 production surfaces, DMP v2 draft/migration surfaces, and any mixed-mode assumptions.
implementation_tasks: Inventory where DMP v1 is produced and consumed live.
Inventory where DMP v2 exists as draft, migration helper, or secondary surface.
State whether any runtime path incorrectly assumes v2 is already canonical.
validation_tasks: Prove the inventory matches current code and tests.
Flag any mixed-mode ambiguity explicitly.
leaves.pass_gate: The repo has a precise inventory of live vs draft DMP surfaces.
leaves.merge_gate: No reader can reasonably confuse the live runtime packet contract after the decision note lands.

leaves.title: Freeze the live DMP surface for the workflow-modification tranche
leaves.scope: Make the live DMP choice explicit for the workflow-modification tranche and prevent accidental partial migration.
implementation_tasks: Record whether DMP v1 remains the canonical live packet surface for this tranche or whether a dedicated promotion is occurring.
Document explicit non-goals if v2 is not promoted.
Add or tighten tests to block silent mixed-mode drift.
validation_tasks: Prove the chosen DMP decision is reflected consistently in docs and tests.
leaves.pass_gate: The DMP choice for this tranche is explicit, bounded, and test-enforced.
leaves.merge_gate: There is no ambiguity about whether runtime is coded for legacy DMP, v1, or v2.

leaves.title: If DMP v2 promotion is chosen, emit a bounded successor promotion pack instead of mixed-mode implementation
leaves.scope: Prevent Gate 54 from smuggling a live DMP v2 promotion into unrelated workflow work by forcing a separate successor plan if v2 becomes the chosen direction.
implementation_tasks: State explicitly whether Gate 54 ends with a v1 freeze or with a successor DMP v2 promotion plan.
If v2 promotion is selected, write a bounded successor pack covering producer surfaces, migration tests, fixture upgrades, and backward-compatibility rules.
State that no partial live v2 implementation is authorised inside Gate 54 itself.
validation_tasks: Ensure the decision note and successor pack agree on whether v2 is live, draft, or pending promotion.
Ensure no source-code implementation is slipped into this decision leaf.
leaves.pass_gate: Gate 54 ends with an explicit bounded DMP outcome rather than an ambiguous mixed-mode state.
leaves.merge_gate: If DMP v2 is chosen, a successor promotion pack exists before any implementation branch starts.

leaves.title: Align vocabulary governance to family/variant/expression/horizon ownership
leaves.scope: Rebase vocabulary governance onto the now-pinned architecture so terminology tracks actual stage ownership and hierarchy.
implementation_tasks: Add canonical ownership for family, setup variant, execution expression, and horizon terms.
Align code slugs and planning labels with canonical terminology.
Preserve allowed aliases and document deprecations.
validation_tasks: Prove generated vocabulary artefacts match checked-in canonical files.
Prove conflicting duplicate labels are blocked.
leaves.pass_gate: Vocabulary governance now reflects the updated workflow architecture rather than stale prior terms.
leaves.merge_gate: No conflicting duplicate labels or undefined aliases remain in the canonical governance surface.

leaves.title: Add governance enforcement for future workflow expansion
leaves.scope: Prevent future workflow and playbook expansion from silently introducing conflicting labels or unowned terms.
implementation_tasks: Add enforcement checks for duplicate labels, stale aliases, and workflow terms lacking canonical ownership.
Document the promotion path for new family or setup-variant terms.
validation_tasks: Prove enforcement tests fail on deliberate drift cases and pass on the canonical set.
leaves.pass_gate: Future workflow expansion is governance-checked rather than relying on memory and luck.
leaves.merge_gate: Vocabulary and workflow governance are enforceable, not advisory only.
